etymology.description: 晚(晚) (wǎn, "evening; late") and 上(上) (shàng, "up; above; on") combine to depict the concept of evening.
etymology.interpretation: 晚(晚) (wǎn, "evening; late") denotes the time of day after sunset, when activities typically slow down. 上(上) (shàng, "up; above; on") can give the sense of something being on or upon, here suggesting the time that follows the day being 'upon' us, in the sense of happening now. Together, 晚上 ('evening') describes the period of transition from day to night when it becomes late.

examples.chinese: 晚上(晚上)，我们常常去散步(我們常常去散步)。
examples.english: In the evening, we often go for a walk.

examples.chinese: 晚上(晚上)，星星在天上闪烁(星星在天上閃爍)。
examples.pinyin: Wǎnshang, xīngxing zài tiān shàng shǎnshuò.
examples.english: In the evening, the stars twinkle in the sky.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢晚上喝热茶(我喜歡晚上喝熱茶)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān wǎnshang hē rè chá.
examples.english: I like drinking hot tea in the evening.
